metadata.title: Score a Build Plan Using the Boondoggle Score with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: You made the highest-risk row the one every other step inherits from - step 3, state persistence - and rejected 'Claude completes the data model' because it is not machine-checkable. I want to build a real Boondoggle Score for my own project before I prompt anything. Help me: (1) turn each planned step's handoff condition into an assertion that either exits zero or fails, (2) find which step every other step inherits from and justify why it carries the most risk, and (3) show the concrete pytest condition that would replace a vague 'Claude finishes' row.

props.command: You made the highest-risk row the one every other step inherits from - step 3, state persistence - and rejected 'Claude completes the data model' because it is not machine-checkable. I want to build a real Boondoggle Score for my own project before I prompt anything. Help me: (1) turn each planned step's handoff condition into an assertion that either exits zero or fails, (2) find which step every other step inherits from and justify why it carries the most risk, and (3) show the concrete pytest condition that would replace a vague 'Claude finishes' row.
props.segment: Score a Build Plan Using the

beats.narration_text: Before Claude sees a single prompt, you know which step is highest-risk, which handoff is weakest, and which supervisory capacity you're most likely to skip.
A handoff condition must be machine-checkable — it names a test file, a count, or an artifact. Not: Claude finishes. Not: looks good. A specific assertion that exits zero or doesn't.
We ask Claude to generate a Boondoggle Score table for Phase 1 of an application tracker.

props.command:
claude "Generate Boondoggle Score table for app tracker Phase 1:
  columns: step#, phase, labor, supervisory capacity, handoff condition
  flag: critical path and highest-risk handoff
  rule: every handoff condition must be machine-checkable"

beats.narration_text: Claude returns five steps. Every handoff condition names a pytest file or artifact count. The critical path connects the data model to the UI to the state persistence. The highest-risk handoff is step 3 — state persistence is the hardest to test without running the full app.

props.code:
# Boondoggle Score — App Tracker Phase 1

| # | Phase        | Labor  | Capacity | Handoff Condition                        |
|---|--------------|--------|----------|------------------------------------------|
| 1 | Data model   | Claude | —        | pytest tests/test_models.py exits 0      |
| 2 | Add form     | Claude | PA       | pytest tests/test_add.py -k happy        |
| 3 | State pers.  | Claude | PA+IJ    | all 3 apps survive reload in browser     | <- HIGH RISK
| 4 | Delete       | Human  | PF       | pytest tests/test_delete.py exits 0      |
| 5 | UI polish    | Human  | IJ       | screenshot matches wireframe.png         |

Critical path: step 1 -> 2 -> 3 (state persistence blocks all others)

beats.narration_text: The Boondoggle Score table — five steps, critical path highlighted, highest-risk row at step 3. State persistence is the step every other step inherits from.
We deliberately set row 1's handoff condition to 'Claude completes the data model' and re-run the scorer.

props.command: claude "Set row 1 handoff condition to: 'Claude completes the data model'.\nRe-run scorer. Does it flag this condition as invalid?\nExplain why it fails the machine-checkable rule."

beats.narration_text: The validator flags it: not machine-checkable. Then shows the corrected version with pytest.
The Boondoggle Score is a diagnostic before a single line of code is written. The highest-risk row is always the one that every other row inherits from.
Next: run the three-pass verification protocol.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The Boondoggle Score is a diagnostic before a single line of code is written. The highest-risk row is always the one that every other row inherits from. Next: run the three-pass verification protocol.

artifactLines: The validator flags it: not machine-checkable.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Score a Build Plan Using the Boondoggle Score with Claude Code] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
